column4: Validate that the Cognito User Pool issues a valid JWT access token and refresh token on successful authentication
column5: Cognito User Pool deployed; test consultant account created
column6:
1. Invoke auth login via CLI PKCE flow
2. Capture JWT access token
3. Decode and inspect token claims
4. Verify access token expiry is 1 hour
5. Verify refresh token expiry is 30 days
column7: JWT access token issued with correct claims; access expiry 1 hour; refresh token expiry 30 days
column14: Test consultant and admin roles separately

column4: Validate API Gateway rejects requests with invalid or missing JWT tokens
column5: API Gateway deployed with JWT Cognito authoriser
column6:
1. Send request to /api/v1/solutions with no Authorization header
2. Send request with an expired JWT
3. Send request with a tampered JWT payload
4. Send request with an unsigned JWT
column7: All three unauthorized attempts return HTTP 401; no Lambda invocation occurs
column14: Cover all three rejection scenarios per SOW security testing scope

column4: Validate that the global quota counter is correctly initialised to zero for the current month
column5: DynamoDB quotas table deployed; no prior counter entry for current month
column6: 1. Run initialisation script for GLOBAL partition key\n2. Query quotas table for GLOBAL + current month_key\n3. Attempt to initialise again to verify idempotency (condition expression)
column7: Counter exists with value 0; second put-item is rejected by condition expression; no duplicate entries
column14: Use IG Section Application Configuration script verbatim

column4: Validate Agent 0 (Input Validator) rejects a client brief missing required fields
column5: Agent 0 deployed in AgentCore Runtime; test brief with missing fields prepared
column6:
1. Submit brief with missing solution_name field
2. Submit brief with missing client_name field
3. Submit brief with empty discovery questionnaire
4. Verify DynamoDB solution state set to FAILED_VALIDATION
column7: All three briefs are rejected with actionable error messages; solution state is FAILED_VALIDATION; no generation agents invoked
column14: Validate no token spend occurs on invalid inputs

column4: Validate the /api/v1/health endpoint returns 200 with DynamoDB and S3 connectivity status using a valid JWT
column5: API Gateway and all Lambda route handlers deployed; valid JWT available
column6: 1. Send GET /api/v1/health with valid Bearer JWT\n2. Inspect response body for status and service fields\n3. Send GET /api/v1/health without JWT to confirm 401 not enforced on health route
column7: Authenticated request returns HTTP 200 with body containing status:healthy and dynamodb:connected and s3:connected; unauthenticated request also returns 200 (health is unauthenticated per design)
column14: Matches IG Phase 1 step 1.3 expected output

column4: Validate that concurrent generation requests enforce the per-user 10/month quota using atomic conditional writes
column5: DynamoDB quotas table deployed; test consultant user created; quota counter at zero
column6:
1. Submit 15 simultaneous generate requests for the same test user
2. Monitor HTTP response codes
3. Query quotas table for final counter value
4. Verify no race condition allowed more than 10 successes
column7: Exactly 10 requests succeed with HTTP 202; exactly 5 requests return HTTP 429; DynamoDB counter equals 10; no race condition bypass observed
column14: Matches IG quota enforcement test: 15 requests → 10 success + 5 x 429

column4: Validate the EO Validator agent applies deterministic format-check rules and triggers up to 3 retries on failure
column5: All five agents deployed in AgentCore Runtime; known-bad artifact prepared in S3
column6:
1. Place a known-bad artifact (missing required H1 section) in the staging S3 bucket
2. Invoke EO Validator agent against this artifact
3. Verify retry count increments in DynamoDB artifact_statuses map
4. Confirm failure state after 3 retries
5. Place known-good artifact and verify immediate pass
column7: Known-bad artifact triggers up to 3 retries; DynamoDB retry_count reaches 3 before final FAILED status; known-good artifact passes on first attempt with PASSED status
column14: Test both known-good and known-bad fixtures per DD format-check design

column4: Validate the GitHub push Lambda retrieves the PAT from Secrets Manager and commits generated artifacts to the configured repository
column5: GitHub repository provisioned; PAT stored in Secrets Manager; at least one artifact generated and stored in S3
column6:
1. Trigger GitHub push Lambda manually with a test solution_id
2. Verify PAT is retrieved from Secrets Manager (check CloudTrail for GetSecretValue)
3. Verify artifact files appear in GitHub repository with correct commit message format
4. Verify commit SHA is written to DynamoDB solutions table
column7: Artifacts committed to GitHub; commit message contains solution_id and artifact_type; DynamoDB solutions.github_commit_sha populated; PAT not exposed in CloudWatch Logs
column14: Commit message format: solution_id/<artifact_type>: generated by EO Validator

column4: Validate the CLI generate subcommand produces all five presales artifacts end-to-end in the staging environment
column5: All five agents operational; CLI pip package installed; representative brief available
column6:
1. Run eoframework auth login
2. Run eoframework generate --brief representative-brief-001.json --env staging --wait
3. Monitor status until completion
4. Run eoframework artifact list for the solution
5. Download all five presales artifacts
column7: CLI exits with success; all five presales artifacts (solution-briefing.md; statement-of-work.md; discovery-questionnaire.csv; infrastructure-costs.csv; level-of-effort-estimate.csv) are present; each passes EO Validator format-check
column14: This is the Phase 2 exit gate per SOW Milestone M5

column4: Validate the full twelve-artifact solution bundle is generated end-to-end via CLI and all artifacts pass EO Validator format-check and LLM quality-check
column5: All five agents operational in staging; full platform deployed; representative client brief available
column6:
1. Submit generation request via eoframework generate --wait
2. Poll status every 10 seconds until solution state is COMPLETE or PARTIAL
3. Run artifact list to enumerate all twelve artifact types
4. Verify each artifact in DynamoDB artifact_statuses shows PASSED
5. Confirm GitHub commit contains all twelve artifacts
column7: All twelve artifact types present and show PASSED status; generation completes in under 60 minutes; GitHub commit contains all twelve files with correct commit messages; no artifact exhausts all 3 retries on a valid brief
column14: Primary Phase 3 exit gate per SOW; ties to SOW success metric ≤60 min E2E

column4: Validate that API route permissions are enforced per Consultant / Admin / Read-Only roles across all eleven routes
column5: Cognito User Pool live with three test users (one per role); valid JWTs for each role
column6:
1. As Consultant: call POST /api/v1/solutions (expect 202)
2. As Consultant: call GET /api/v1/admin/usage (expect 403)
3. As Admin: call GET /api/v1/admin/usage (expect 200)
4. As Admin: call POST /api/v1/admin/quota-override (expect 200)
5. As Read-Only: call POST /api/v1/solutions (expect 403)
column7: Role enforcement matches DD Role Definitions table exactly; Consultant blocked from admin routes; Read-Only blocked from generate; Admin has full access
column14: Test all three roles against all eleven routes systematically

column3: Quota Counter Integrity After Month Rollover
column4: Validate that the quota counter initialisation is re-executed at the start of each calendar month and does not carry over the previous month count
column5: DynamoDB quotas table with a simulated prior-month entry; current-month entry absent
column6:
1. Simulate month rollover by setting month_key to current month on the GLOBAL counter
2. Run the first generate request of the new month
3. Verify a new GLOBAL counter entry is created for the new month_key with counter starting from 1
4. Verify prior month entry is untouched
column7: New month_key entry created with counter = 1; previous month entry preserved; no quota bypass from prior month bleed-over
column14: Critical regression risk at month boundary; run on first day of each month during hypercare

column3: End-to-End Generation Time at Target Throughput
column4: Validate that the full twelve-artifact solution bundle is produced in under 60 minutes at 10 concurrent generation requests (200 solutions/month peak load)
column5: Staging environment fully deployed; Locust load test scripts configured; representative briefs available
column6:
1. Configure Locust for 10 concurrent users each submitting one generate request
2. Start load test and record submission timestamps
3. Poll solution status endpoints until all solutions reach terminal state
4. Calculate wall-clock elapsed time for each solution from submission to COMPLETE
5. Collect P50 and P95 elapsed time metrics
column7: P95 end-to-end generation time ≤ 60 minutes; zero solutions fail all retries on valid representative briefs; no Lambda timeout errors observed in CloudWatch
column14: SOW success metric: ≤60 min E2E for full 12-artifact bundle; validates cost < $5 per solution

column3: API Gateway P99 Latency — Synchronous Routes
column4: Validate that synchronous API routes respond within the 3-second P99 latency target under load
column5: API Gateway and Lambda deployed in staging; Locust configured for 100 concurrent API calls
column6:
1. Configure Locust to send 100 concurrent GET /api/v1/solutions/{id} and GET /api/v1/auth/me requests
2. Run for 10-minute sustained load
3. Collect API Gateway P50; P95; P99 latency metrics from CloudWatch
4. Verify no 5xx errors during the test window
column7: API Gateway P99 latency ≤ 3 seconds on synchronous routes; error rate < 1%; no Lambda cold-start timeouts observed
column14: SOW SLA: API Gateway P99 ≤ 3 seconds; aligns with DD CloudWatch alarm threshold

column4: Validate that DynamoDB atomic quota conditional write operations complete within 50ms P99 under concurrent load
column5: DynamoDB quotas table deployed On-Demand; quota counter at 0 for test user
column6:
1. Execute 200 concurrent DynamoDB conditional write operations against the quotas table using boto3
2. Measure per-operation latency with timestamps
3. Compute P50; P95; and P99 latencies
4. Verify zero throttle events in CloudWatch
column7: DynamoDB quota conditional write P99 latency < 50ms; zero ThrottledRequests in CloudWatch during the test
column14: SOW SLA: DynamoDB quota op < 50ms P99; DynamoDB On-Demand auto-scales

column4: Validate that the per-solution Bedrock model spend remains below $5 at 200 solutions/month steady-state throughput
column5: Full generation pipeline deployed in staging; DynamoDB token usage tracking active; at least 10 solutions generated
column6:
1. Generate 10 solutions using representative client briefs
2. Query DynamoDB solutions table for per-phase token_usage map for each solution
3. Calculate total estimated cost per solution from input/output token counts using Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5 pricing
4. Extrapolate to 200 solutions/month
column7: Average per-solution Bedrock cost < $5; no single solution exceeds $10; token usage breakdowns available per agent in DynamoDB
column14: SOW success metric: < $5 per solution at 200/month; key ROI validation

column3: Stress Test — Lambda Concurrency and Cold Starts
column4: Identify Lambda cold-start behaviour and determine if provisioned concurrency is required for agent trigger functions
column5: Agent trigger Lambda functions deployed with container images; CloudWatch X-Ray tracing enabled
column6:
1. Scale Lambda concurrency to zero by waiting for idle period
2. Submit 5 simultaneous generate requests to force cold starts
3. Measure P99 duration on agent trigger Lambda functions
4. Evaluate if P99 duration exceeds 80% of 900-second timeout
5. If threshold exceeded enable provisioned concurrency and retest
column7: Cold-start P99 duration < 720 seconds (80% of 900s timeout); if threshold is exceeded provisioned concurrency must be enabled and the test repeated to confirm resolution
column14: DD recommends evaluating provisioned concurrency if P99 > 80% of timeout; Phase 3 gate

column3: JWT Penetration Testing — Token Tampering and Replay
column4: Validate that expired; tampered; and unsigned JWT tokens are rejected by the API Gateway JWT Cognito authoriser on all eleven routes
column5: Staging API Gateway deployed; valid and expired JWTs prepared for test; security testing tools configured
column6:
1. Send a valid JWT to GET /api/v1/solutions — expect 200
2. Wait for access token to expire (or modify exp claim) — send same token — expect 401
3. Modify the JWT sub claim and send — expect 401
4. Strip the JWT signature and send — expect 401
5. Use a JWT issued by a different Cognito pool — expect 401
column7: All four unauthorized scenarios return HTTP 401; no Lambda function is invoked; CloudTrail logs show authoriser rejection for each attempt; no 200 responses for invalid tokens
column14: SOW security test: JWT auth penetration; aligns with IG Security Validation checklist

column4: Validate that the DynamoDB atomic conditional write prevents per-user quota bypass via concurrent batch requests
column5: DynamoDB quotas table deployed; test user with counter at 8 (2 remaining); race condition test script prepared
column6:
1. Send 20 simultaneous POST /api/v1/solutions requests using the same test user JWT
2. Verify total successful responses (HTTP 202) does not exceed 2 (the remaining quota)
3. Verify all remaining requests return HTTP 429
4. Query DynamoDB quotas table to confirm final counter value is exactly 10
column7: Maximum 2 solutions succeed regardless of concurrency; all remaining requests return HTTP 429; DynamoDB counter equals 10; zero quota bypass incidents
column14: SOW security test: quota bypass scenarios; zero bypass incidents is a go-live gate

column4: Validate that the GitHub Personal Access Token is not exposed in Lambda logs; CloudWatch Logs; or API response payloads
column5: GitHub push Lambda deployed; PAT stored in Secrets Manager; at least one successful GitHub commit executed
column6:
1. Trigger the GitHub push Lambda by completing a full generation
2. Inspect CloudWatch Logs for the eofw-{ENV}-fn-github-push log group for any PAT string
3. Inspect all API response payloads from /api/v1/solutions/{id} for any credential strings
4. Verify CloudTrail shows GetSecretValue call with correct requester identity (Lambda execution role only)
column7: PAT string is absent from all CloudWatch Log entries; PAT absent from all API response payloads; CloudTrail GetSecretValue events show only the GitHub push Lambda execution role as requester
column14: SOW security test: Secrets Manager PAT protection; go-live gate per security test report

column3: IAM Privilege Escalation and Wildcard Policy Review
column4: Validate that no Lambda execution role contains wildcard IAM actions or resource ARNs using IAM Access Analyzer
column5: All 17 Lambda IAM execution roles deployed; IAM Access Analyzer enabled in us-west-2
column6:
1. Run aws accessanalyzer list-findings for the account-level analyzer
2. Filter for ACTIVE findings
3. Manually inspect all 17 Lambda execution role IAM policies for wildcard * in Action or Resource
4. Verify S3 bucket public access block is enabled (all four settings)
column7: IAM Access Analyzer reports zero active findings; no Lambda execution role contains wildcard Action or Resource ARN; S3 bucket public access block shows all four settings as true
column14: SOW least-privilege requirement; go-live gate; aligns with IG security quality gate

column4: Validate that the agent Docker image in ECR contains no CRITICAL CVE findings before production go-live
column5: ECR repository with latest agent image deployed; Amazon Inspector ECR scanning enabled
column6:
1. Trigger an Amazon Inspector scan on the latest agent image tag in ECR
2. Review scan findings for CRITICAL and HIGH severity CVEs
3. If CRITICAL findings exist remediate base image or dependency and rebuild
4. Confirm rescan shows zero CRITICAL findings
column7: Zero CRITICAL CVE findings in the ECR agent image scan results; HIGH findings documented and accepted or remediated before go-live
column14: SOW security scope: ECR image scanning; CRITICAL findings block production go-live

column3: API Availability — Lambda Error Rate Baseline
column4: Validate that the platform maintains API availability ≥ 99.5% with Lambda error rate below the 1% alarm threshold during a sustained 24-hour observation window
column5: All 17 Lambda functions deployed; CloudWatch alarms configured; staging environment receiving simulated load
column6:
1. Submit 50 generate requests evenly spaced over a 24-hour period in staging
2. Monitor Lambda error rate CloudWatch alarm for the entire 24-hour window
3. Monitor API Gateway 5xx error rate
4. Calculate availability percentage from total invocations vs errors
column7: Lambda error rate < 1% throughout the 24-hour window; API availability ≥ 99.5%; zero P1 CloudWatch alarms fire during the observation period
column14: SOW success metric: API availability ≥ 99.5% monthly; CloudWatch alarm threshold: error rate > 1%

column4: Validate that the DynamoDB Point-In-Time Recovery restore procedure completes within the 4-hour RTO target
column5: DynamoDB PITR enabled on all four tables; test data present in solutions and quotas tables
column6:
1. Record current timestamp (T0)
2. Delete a DynamoDB table row to simulate data loss
3. Initiate PITR restore to a point-in-time 1 hour prior to T0
4. Measure elapsed time from restore initiation to table availability
5. Validate data integrity of restored table against expected records
column7: DynamoDB table restored to target point-in-time in < 4 hours; restored table data matches expected records; integrity validation script passes with zero mismatches
column14: SOW RTO target: 4 hours; mandatory Phase 3 go-live gate per SOW go-live readiness checklist

column4: Validate that the CloudWatch Synthetics canary successfully polls /api/v1/health every 5 minutes and triggers a P1 alarm on consecutive failures
column5: CloudWatch Synthetics canary deployed and running; SNS ops alerts topic with confirmed email subscription
column6:
1. Verify canary is in RUNNING state
2. Inspect last 12 canary run results (1 hour) — expect all PASSED
3. Simulate a health check failure by temporarily disabling the health-check Lambda
4. Verify canary failure alarm fires after 2 consecutive failed runs
5. Restore health-check Lambda and verify alarm clears
column7: Canary shows PASSED on all runs during normal operation; alarm fires within 10 minutes of 2 consecutive failures; SNS notification received by Daniel Park; alarm clears after Lambda restored
column14: DD CloudWatch Synthetics: every 5 minutes; P1 alarm on ≥2 consecutive failures

column3: CloudTrail Audit Logging — API and S3 Data Events
column4: Validate that CloudTrail captures all management events and S3 data events on the artifact bucket with the required fields
column5: CloudTrail deployed with management events and S3 data events enabled; CloudTrail S3 bucket with Object Lock; at least one API call and one S3 PutObject executed
column6:
1. Execute a generate request via CLI
2. Download a generated artifact (triggers S3 GetObject)
3. Query CloudTrail for events within the last 15 minutes
4. Verify management events include Lambda invocations with requester identity and source IP
5. Verify S3 data events include PutObject and GetObject on the artifact bucket
column7: CloudTrail management events present with eventSource; eventTime; userIdentity.arn; and sourceIPAddress fields; S3 PutObject and GetObject data events present for artifact bucket; CloudTrail S3 bucket has Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode
column14: SOC 2 CC7.2; SOC 2 CC8.1 requirement; CloudTrail is an immutable WORM audit trail

column3: DynamoDB Audit Events Retention and TTL
column4: Validate that all platform API actions are logged to the audit_events DynamoDB table and that the 90-day TTL attribute is correctly set
column5: DynamoDB audit_events table deployed with TTL enabled on the ttl attribute; several platform actions executed
column6:
1. Execute five distinct API actions (generate; status; list; artifact download; admin usage)
2. Query audit_events table for the test user_id
3. Verify each action has a record with user_id; action; solution_id; timestamp; outcome; and source_ip fields
4. Verify each record has a ttl attribute set to current epoch + 90 days
column7: All five actions are logged with required fields; TTL attribute is present and set to epoch timestamp approximately 90 days in the future; DynamoDB TTL setting is enabled on the table
column14: SOC 2 CC6.3; 90-day audit log retention per data governance policy

column3: Full Presales Bundle Generation via CLI
column4: Validate that a consultant can generate the complete five-artifact presales bundle from a representative client brief using the CLI generate subcommand with no human intervention in the generation loop
column5: UAT environment fully deployed; representative client brief provided by Marcus Patel; CLI installed and authenticated on consultant laptop
column6:
1. Login as a consultant user via eoframework auth login
2. Submit brief using eoframework generate --brief representative-brief-001.json --env production --wait
3. Monitor progress via eoframework status <solution_id> until COMPLETE
4. Run eoframework artifact list to enumerate all five presales artifacts
5. Download and review solution-briefing.md and statement-of-work.md for content accuracy
6. Confirm GitHub commit exists in the production repository
column7: All five presales artifacts generated; solution state is COMPLETE; generation completed in under 60 minutes; artifacts are coherent and reference the client context from the brief; GitHub commit is present with correct commit message
column14: Primary UAT scenario for Marcus Patel sign-off; go-live gate per SOW UAT sign-off requirement

column3: Full Delivery Bundle Generation — All 12 Artifacts
column4: Validate that the platform generates all twelve EO Framework artifacts (five presales + six delivery + one Terraform automation bundle) end-to-end and each artifact passes EO Validator quality check
column5: UAT environment fully deployed; representative client brief available; Phase 3 validation complete
column6:
1. Submit a full generation request via CLI with a representative brief
2. Poll status until solution reaches COMPLETE or PARTIAL state
3. Inspect DynamoDB artifact_statuses map to confirm all twelve artifact types show PASSED
4. Download at least three delivery artifacts and review content quality
5. Confirm Terraform automation bundle is present in S3 automation prefix
6. Confirm all twelve artifacts committed to GitHub repository
column7: All twelve artifacts present with PASSED validation status; generation time ≤ 60 minutes; Terraform bundle is present in S3; GitHub contains all twelve committed files; no artifact requires manual remediation
column14: Validates SOW success metric: all 12 artifact types pass format-check + LLM quality-check with ≤3 retries; executive demo scenario

column3: CLI Status — Per-Phase Token Usage Display
column4: Validate that the CLI status subcommand displays per-phase Bedrock token usage (input tokens; output tokens; estimated cost) for a completed solution
column5: Full generation pipeline operational; at least one completed solution with token usage data recorded in DynamoDB
column6:
1. Run eoframework generate and wait for completion
2. Run eoframework status <solution_id>
3. Inspect the CLI output for per-agent token usage section
4. Verify input tokens; output tokens; and estimated cost (USD) are shown for Pre-Sales Generator; Delivery Generator; Code Generator; and EO Validator phases
5. Cross-check displayed cost against DynamoDB solutions table token_usage map
column7: Status output shows per-phase token usage with input tokens; output tokens; and estimated cost for each generation agent; total estimated cost is under $5 for a full 12-artifact bundle
column14: SOW UAT scenario: CLI status with per-phase token usage; demonstrates cost governance capability to PREDICTif leadership

column4: Validate that an Admin user can override a consultant's per-user monthly quota via the admin API endpoint and the consultant can immediately generate an additional solution
column5: Admin user with valid JWT; test consultant user with quota at 10/10 (exhausted); UAT environment deployed
column6:
1. Authenticate as Admin user via CLI
2. Confirm test consultant receives HTTP 429 on generate request
3. Call POST /api/v1/admin/quota-override with consultant user_id and new limit of 12
4. Re-attempt generate request as consultant
5. Verify generate succeeds with HTTP 202
6. Verify quota override event recorded in DynamoDB audit_events table
column7: Quota override succeeds; consultant can generate after override; solution proceeds to completion; audit_events table contains override record with admin user_id; action; and timestamp
column14: SOW UAT scenario: admin quota override; demonstrates administrative control required for managing the 120-consultant user base

column4: Validate that all generated and passing artifacts are automatically committed to the configured GitHub repository with the correct commit message format after each solution completes
column5: GitHub repository provisioned; PAT stored in Secrets Manager; at least one complete solution generated in production
column6:
1. Complete a full solution generation via CLI in production
2. Navigate to the GitHub repository in the browser
3. Locate the commit corresponding to the solution_id
4. Verify commit message follows format: solution_id/<artifact_type>: generated by EO Validator
5. Verify all passing artifact files are present under the correct solution_id directory in the repository
6. Verify commit is attributed to the platform PAT (not a personal account)
column7: All passing artifacts are present in the GitHub repository under the solution_id directory; commit message format is correct for each file; commit author matches the Secrets Manager PAT account; Daniel Park confirms the commit is visible
column14: Validates automated GitHub delivery pipeline; key operational handover requirement for Daniel Park

column4: Validate that the CloudWatch health dashboard provides real-time operational visibility into Lambda error rates; Bedrock token usage; and solution generation throughput as required for the green metrics baseline
column5: CloudWatch dashboard deployed; at least 5 solutions generated in production; all CloudWatch alarms in OK state
column6:
1. Open the eofw-prd-health CloudWatch dashboard in the AWS Console
2. Review the Lambda Error Rate widget — confirm zero errors in last 1 hour
3. Review the Bedrock Token Usage — Estimated Cost widget — confirm cost data is populating
4. Review the API Gateway P99 latency widget
5. Confirm all seven CloudWatch alarms are in OK state
6. Daniel Park navigates all widgets independently and confirms understanding
column7: Dashboard displays all key metrics without errors; Lambda error rate widget shows 0%; Bedrock cost widget shows per-agent estimated cost data; all alarms in OK state; Daniel Park confirms dashboard is operationally useful and no training gaps identified
column14: SOW go-live gate: green CloudWatch metrics baseline confirmed; validates executive demo readiness

column3: Artifact Validation Pass Rate — 95% First-Attempt Target
column4: Validate that the artifact validation first-attempt pass rate is ≥95% across all twelve artifact types using ten representative client briefs
column5: Ten representative client briefs provided by Marcus Patel; full platform deployed in staging; DynamoDB validation metrics available
column6:
1. Submit ten generation requests using the ten representative client briefs
2. After all solutions complete; query DynamoDB for first-attempt pass/fail status per artifact per solution
3. Calculate the first-attempt pass rate: (artifacts that passed on retry_count = 0) / (total artifacts generated) × 100
4. Confirm overall pass rate meets or exceeds 95%
column7: First-attempt artifact validation pass rate ≥ 95% across all twelve artifact types over the ten representative briefs; any artifact falling below 95% is investigated and prompt or format-check adjustments are made before go-live
column14: SOW success metric: ≥95% validation pass rate on first attempt; this metric gates production go-live approval

column4: Validate the platform is demonstrable to executive sponsor Sarah Lin covering all four core capabilities: CLI auth; presales generation; delivery generation; and Terraform automation bundle
column5: Production environment fully deployed and green; all other UAT tests passed; executive demo brief prepared; Marcus Patel UAT sign-off obtained; CTO sign-off obtained
column6:
1. Sarah Lin and Marcus Patel observe the demonstration
2. Authenticate via eoframework auth login (demonstrates CLI + Cognito)
3. Submit generation request with a representative brief (demonstrates async generation)
4. Display real-time status updates with per-phase token usage
5. Show completed solution with all twelve artifacts including Terraform bundle in GitHub
6. Open CloudWatch dashboard to show green metrics baseline
7. Demonstrate admin quota override capability
column7: Executive demonstration is delivered successfully; all four core capabilities shown end-to-end; Sarah Lin confirms the platform meets the executive-level acceptance criteria for Q2 2026; formal go-live approval is obtained from CTO
column14: SOW Milestone: Go-Live — executive demo delivered to Sarah Lin; mandatory final gate before platform handover to Amatra team
